title: The Crow and the Pitcher
lines: A thirsty Crow found a pitcher with a little water in the bottom.
His beak was too short to reach the water.
He tried and tried, but in vain.
Then an idea came to him.
Picking up some small pebbles, he dropped them into the pitcher one by one.
With each pebble the water rose a little higher.
At last it was near enough so that he could drink.
moral: Little by little does the trick.
